description: In C, validate external input at the argv parsing, fread/fgets bounds, and ntohl/htonl on wire data before internal use.
body: In C, parsing, environment, CLI, and network inputs reach the program through argv parsing, fread/fgets bounds, and ntohl/htonl on wire data and must be checked before they are trusted. Use when handling untrusted or external input crossing into C code. Do not pass raw external values into internal logic, and avoid assuming well-formed input. Actions: validate at the boundary, reject or normalize malformed values, verify the rejection path with a test, and record the accepted contract. Validation signals include tests on malformed input, a logged/trace rejection, and a defined schema for the boundary. Risk boundary: escalate when input feeds security-sensitive or non-reversible operations; deny by default rather than guessing intent.
